prompts.text: Create a workflow that receives an HTTP POST webhook at /contacts, validates the email field is present, and returns a success response with the contact ID.
Create a workflow triggered by a cron schedule every day at 9am that fetches a list of users from an API endpoint and logs the count.
Create a workflow with an HTTP GET endpoint at /health that checks a database connection status and returns either "healthy" or "unhealthy" with the current timestamp.

results.prompt: Create a workflow that receives an HTTP POST webhook at /contacts, validates the email field is present, and returns a success response with the contact ID.

checklist.item: Has HTTP POST webhook trigger at /contacts
Validates that the email field is present in the request body
Branches based on whether the email field is present or missing
Returns a success response containing the contact ID when email is present
Returns an error or rejection response when email field is missing

checklistResults.reasoning: The Webhook Trigger node has httpMethod set to 'POST' and path set to 'contacts', which corresponds to a POST webhook at /contacts
The 'Validate Email' IF node checks whether '$json.body.email' exists using the 'string exists' operator, which validates the presence of the email field
The IF node branches into two paths: the true branch (index 0) goes to 'Success Response' when email exists, and the false branch (index 1) goes to 'Error Response' when email is missing
The Success Response node returns a JSON body containing 'success: true' and a 'contactId' field (generated from $runIndex and $now.toMillis()), along with the email, when the email field is present
The Error Response node returns a JSON body with 'success: false' and an error message 'Email field is required', with HTTP status code 400, when the email field is missing

results.prompt: Create a workflow triggered by a cron schedule every day at 9am that fetches a list of users from an API endpoint and logs the count.

checklist.item: Has a cron/scheduled trigger that runs daily at 9am
Makes an HTTP request to fetch a list of users from an API endpoint
Logs or outputs the count of users returned from the API

checklistResults.reasoning: The scheduleTrigger node 'Daily at 9am' is configured with field 'days', daysInterval 1, triggerAtHour 9, and triggerAtMinute 0, which runs daily at 9am.
The 'Fetch Users' node uses n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest with method GET and url 'https://api.example.com/users', which fetches a list of users from an API endpoint.
The 'Log User Count' code node computes the count via $input.all().length, logs it with console.log(`Total users: ${count}`), and returns it as output.

results.prompt: Create a workflow with an HTTP GET endpoint at /health that checks a database connection status and returns either "healthy" or "unhealthy" with the current timestamp.

checklist.item: Has an HTTP GET trigger at the /health path
Includes a step that checks a database connection status
Branches based on whether the database connection check succeeds or fails
Returns 'healthy' status when the database connection check succeeds
Returns 'unhealthy' status when the database connection check fails
Response includes the current timestamp regardless of health status
Sends an HTTP response back to the caller with the health check result

checklistResults.reasoning: The webhook node has httpMethod set to 'GET' and path set to 'health', which corresponds to a GET trigger at /health
The 'Check Database Connection' node uses n8n-nodes-base.postgres with a 'SELECT 1 as status' query to check the database connection

The postgres node uses 'onError: continueErrorOutput', and connections show the main output goes to 'Build Healthy Response' while the error output goes to 'Build Unhealthy Response', creating a branch based on success or failure
The 'Build Healthy Response' node sets status to 'healthy' when the database connection check succeeds
The 'Build Unhealthy Response' node sets status to 'unhealthy' when the database connection check fails
Both 'Build Healthy Response' and 'Build Unhealthy Response' nodes include a timestamp field set to '={{ $now.toISO() }}', ensuring the current timestamp is included in both cases
Both 'Return Healthy' and 'Return Unhealthy' nodes use n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook to send an HTTP response back to the caller with the health check result
